skill.name: explore-run
skill.description: Plans bounded exploratory runs for deep learning research repositories. It ranks candidate variants, keeps results separate from trusted baselines, and records caveats for human review.
skill.summary: Plan and summarize isolated exploratory deep learning runs.
skill.author: lllllllama

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, path traversal, and weak cryptography patterns, but review found these were mostly markdown text, relative documentation links, and ordinary descriptions. No malicious intent, network access, credential access, or prompt injection was found. The remaining risk is low because helper scripts read and write local experiment planning files.

low_findings.title: Local File Read and Write Helper Scripts
low_findings.description: The helper script accepts a required spec path and optional output path, then reads and writes JSON locally. This is legitimate for experiment planning, but users should avoid passing sensitive or unrelated paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file access is explicit and local. The risk is limited because there is no network transfer, shell execution, or secret collection.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Matches Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported command execution locations are inline markdown references, output file names, or policy text. I did not find evidence of shell backtick execution in these files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched text is markdown inline code or documentation prose. There is no Ruby code, shell backtick operator, or executable command string at these locations.

low_findings.title: Static Path Traversal Matches Are Relative Reference False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported traversal sequences are documentation references to shared skill files. They are not used as runtime user-controlled paths in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The traversal-like strings appear only in markdown references. There is no code on these lines that opens, writes, uploads, or executes those paths.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Matches Are Text False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged weak cryptography locations contain descriptions, selection weighting text, or argument parser text. I did not find cryptographic operations at those lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines do not instantiate MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or another weak primitive. They are ordinary prose or CLI help text.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Specified Local Output Path
dangerous_patterns.description: The plan variant helper can write generated JSON to a caller-supplied path. This is useful, but callers should keep outputs inside the intended experiment workspace.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The write path is directly controlled by a command-line argument. The impact is bounded to local file writes in the executing environment.

content.value_statement: Research teams need fast experiment probes without confusing them with trusted baselines. This skill plans bounded variants, ranks candidates, and writes clear evidence summaries.
actual_capabilities: Plans exploratory run candidates only after explicit authorization.
Ranks variants using cost, predicted success, and expected gain.
Supports subset sizes, short run steps, and configurable variant axes.
Generates structured local summaries under explore_outputs.
Separates exploratory evidence from trusted baseline verification.
limitations: It does not certify SOTA claims or trusted training success.
It does not orchestrate a full current_research exploration workflow.
It depends on repository-specific commands supplied by the user.
It writes local files and should run inside the intended workspace.

use_cases.title: Rank Small Experiment Probes
use_cases.description: Select a small set of low-cost training probes before committing to longer runs.

use_cases.title: Compare Variant Axes Safely
use_cases.description: Explore learning rates, augmentations, or model options while keeping evidence separate from baseline results.

use_cases.title: Prepare Reviewable Run Summaries
use_cases.description: Produce concise exploratory summaries that show caveats, comparability limits, and top run candidates.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Small Probe
prompt_templates.prompt: Use explore-run to plan a small-subset validation for this repository. Keep the run isolated and summarize expected evidence only.

prompt_templates.title: Rank Candidate Variants
prompt_templates.prompt: Use explore-run with these variant axes, subset sizes, and short run steps. Rank candidates by cost, success rate, and expected gain.

prompt_templates.title: Apply Custom Selection Weights
prompt_templates.prompt: Use explore-run with custom selection weights. Favor lower cost while preserving enough expected gain for a useful probe.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize Executed Exploratory Runs
prompt_templates.prompt: Use explore-run to summarize completed exploratory runs. Label unfair comparisons clearly and avoid trusted success claims.

output_examples.input: Plan three short-cycle candidates for a new augmentation strategy.
output_examples.output: A ranked list of candidate runs with estimated cost, expected gain, and notes on why each comparison is bounded.

output_examples.input: Summarize exploratory results after two subset runs finish.
output_examples.output: A short evidence summary that identifies the top run, documents caveats, and separates results from the trusted baseline.

output_examples.input: Rebalance selection toward cheaper probes for an idle GPU window.
output_examples.output: A pruned candidate set that favors low runtime while preserving at least one higher-gain option.

best_practices: Use explicit authorization before starting exploratory planning.
Keep experiment outputs in explore_outputs and separate from trusted baselines.
State comparability limits whenever subset sizes or run lengths differ.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for verified benchmark claims.
Do not promote exploratory results into trusted reports automatically.
Do not pass sensitive files or unrelated paths to helper scripts.

faq.question: Does this skill run training commands by itself?
faq.answer: No. It plans and summarizes exploratory runs, and it may hand execution to another appropriate runner.

faq.question: Can it prove a model is better than a baseline?
faq.answer: No. It produces bounded evidence and requires human review before any trusted claim.

faq.question: Where does it place outputs?
faq.answer: The skill expects local summaries in explore_outputs, including changesets, comparability notes, top runs, and status data.

faq.question: What inputs does variant planning need?
faq.answer: It needs a baseline reference, variant axes, subset sizes, short run steps, budgets, and a primary metric.

faq.question: Is this suitable for production model deployment?
faq.answer: No. It is for research exploration, not production validation, compliance review, or release approval.

faq.question: What should users check before publishing results?
faq.answer: Users should verify fairness, run length, dataset subset, metric direction, and baseline consistency.
